nativeRange.habitat: Sand and rubble zones on steep outer reef slopes
nativeRange.biotope: Deep outer reef slopes around Kwajalein Atoll, typically reported at around 40–100 m depth

diet.notes: Generally accepts vitamin-enriched frozen mysis and brine shrimp, finely chopped meaty preparations, and high-quality marine flake or pellets. Feeding multiple small meals per day is commonly advised given the species' active mid-water foraging. Has been documented feeding on zooplankton and small crustaceans in the water column.

avoidWith: other male fairy wrasses in the same tank

summary: A deep-reef fairy wrasse from the Marshall Islands with a gold body, blue head, and diamond-patterned tail; generally considered peaceful and reef-safe with caution in medium reef aquariums.
careNotes: Cirrhilabrus rhomboidalis is reported only from the Marshall Islands (notably Kwajalein Atoll), where it inhabits steep outer reef slopes over sand and rubble at roughly 40–100 m depth, feeding on zooplankton. Sources disagree on adult size — Quality Marine cites about 3 in (~7.6 cm), LiveAquaria cites a maximum of 4.5 in (~11.4 cm), and FishBase records 8.5 cm TL — most aquarium specimens arrive at 7–10 cm. Tank-size guidance also diverges: Quality Marine suggests around 50 gal (~190 L) while LiveAquaria recommends 90 gal (~340 L). We have leaned toward the larger end given the species' active mid-water swimming, the value of a long footprint with open swimming lanes, and the playbook's cautious-larger rule for tank sizing. Generally regarded as reef-safe, though has been known to pick at very small ornamental crustaceans, so caution is warranted with delicate shrimp species. Like most Cirrhilabrus, this fish is a notable jumper — a tightly fitted lid or eurobrace is strongly recommended. Best kept as a single male, or as a male with one or more females added simultaneously; multiple males in the same tank tend to fight. Individuals are protogynous (female-first sequential hermaphrodites), and males display intensified color during nuptial displays. Coming from deeper reef habitats, the species is commonly considered to acclimate well to subdued lighting and benefits from being introduced before larger or more boisterous tankmates.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: LiveAquaria species page used for tank size (90 gal), water parameters, reef-safe rating, diet, origin, and maximum size (4.5 in). Located via search after the wrasse-reef-safe collection page did not list this species.

additional.name: qualitymarine.com
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing (~3 in / ~7.6 cm), tank size (50 gal / ~190 L), water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025), temperament, and the 'may pick at small crustaceans' reef-safe caveat. Tank-size disagreement with LiveAquaria noted; resolved with cautious-larger bias to ~300 L.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed family Labridae, order Eupercaria, max length 8.5 cm TL (common 6.7 cm SL), distribution Marshall Islands (Kwajalein Atoll), depth range 40–100 m, reef-associated.
